title: trifolium repens var. nevadense
intro: Trifolium repens var. nevadense, also known as Nevada white clover, is a variety of white clover that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It is a perennial legume with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including skin conditions and respiratory problems. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in herbal preparations to provide relief from inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Trifolium repens var. nevadense can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in traditional folk medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
